terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: live event alli line by line
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: Refers to an execution model where code is translated and executed line-by-line, analogous to a live human translator at a conference.

terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: recorded event ide... total event na convert māḍi
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: Refers to an execution model where the entire source code is translated into machine code at once before execution, analogous to translating a pre-recorded video.

terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: uniform bēku... clothes type yāvāglū static āgirutte
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: A programming language rule where variable data types must be explicitly declared and cannot change, compared to a strict school uniform policy.

terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: no uniform... yāvattādru bēkādāg change māḍbōdu
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: A programming language rule where variables do not require explicit type declaration and can change types during runtime, compared to wearing casual clothes.

terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: template na nāvu create māḍkōtīvi... car blueprint
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: A blueprint or template used in Object-Oriented Programming to define the structure and behavior of objects.

terminology_glossary.code_mixed_phrase: ā template iṁda māḍō yāvudē product... Tata, BMW, Benz
terminology_glossary.contextual_meaning: An instance created from a class blueprint in Object-Oriented Programming, compared to specific car brands built from a general car blueprint.

concepts.explanation: Understanding that just as humans use languages like English to communicate with each other, developers use programming languages to give instructions to computers.
Computers only understand binary (0s and 1s). High-level programming languages must be converted into this machine code using a compiler or interpreter.
Interpreters translate and execute code line-by-line (slower but easier to debug), while compilers translate the entire program at once into an executable file.
In Python, variables do not need a predefined data type (like int or string). The type is assigned automatically at runtime and can be changed dynamically.
A programming paradigm based on creating templates (classes) to generate specific instances (objects), which improves code modularity and reusability.
Using an Integrated Development Environment (IDE) like VS Code to write and execute basic code, such as using the print() function to display text.
